subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Help the Story Solver Crew match math equations to everyday problems.
lesson.mini_lesson: Word problems tell a short story with known numbers and an unknown. Decide whether to add, subtract, compare, or do two steps.
lesson.objective: Solve one-step and two-step addition and subtraction word problems within 100.
lesson.concept_explanation: Use the words and model to decide the operation. Unknowns can be the result, a part, or the starting amount.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may choose an operation from one keyword instead of reading the whole story.
lesson.real_world_connection: Word problems help solve questions about supplies, scores, collections, and lengths.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read, model, write an equation, solve, and check the answer against the story.
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

worked_examples.text: Mia has 24 shells and gets 18 more, so 24 + 18 = 42.
A jar had 65 marbles and 19 rolled out, so 65 - 19 = 46.
For two steps, solve the first action before the second action.

hint_ladder: Read the story and find what is asked.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

misconception_bank.operation_selection_error: Learner may add when the story calls for subtraction, or subtract when the story calls for adding.
misconception_bank.missing_unknown_confusion: Learner may not notice whether the unknown is the start, change, or result.
misconception_bank.compare_problem_confusion: Learner may add both compared amounts instead of finding the difference.
misconception_bank.two_step_tracking_error: Learner may answer after only one step or lose the intermediate result.

game_theme.title: Addition and Subtraction Word Problems
game_theme.mission_intro: Help the Story Solver Crew match math equations to everyday problems.

guided_practice.prompt: Mia has 24 shells. Leo gives her 18 more. How many shells does Mia have now?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

guided_practice.explanation: 24 + 18 = 42. Add to put together or find the missing addend.

question_audio.text: Mia has 24 shells. Leo gives her 18 more. How many shells does Mia have now?
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.prompt: There are 36 red apples and 27 green apples. How many apples are there altogether?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

guided_practice.explanation: 36 + 27 = 63. Add to put together or find the missing addend.

question_audio.text: There are 36 red apples and 27 green apples. How many apples are there altogether?
question_audio.label: Read Question

Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

guided_practice.explanation: 29 + 31 = 60. Add to put together or find the missing addend.

question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: A class collected 24 cans Monday and 31 cans Tuesday. They recycled 18 cans. How many cans are left?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Solve one step, then use that result for the next step: 24 + 31 - 18 = 37.

question_audio.text: A class collected 24 cans Monday and 31 cans Tuesday. They recycled 18 cans. How many cans are left?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Ben had 70 stickers. He gave 25 away and then got 14 more. How many stickers now?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Solve one step, then use that result for the next step: 70 - 25 + 14 = 59.

question_audio.text: Ben had 70 stickers. He gave 25 away and then got 14 more. How many stickers now?
question_audio.label: Read Question

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: A bus had 64 riders. 18 got off and 23 got on. How many riders are on the bus?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.

Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Solve one step, then use that result for the next step: 64 - 18 + 23 = 69.

question_audio.text: A bus had 64 riders. 18 got off and 23 got on. How many riders are on the bus?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Lily read 28 pages Saturday and 26 Sunday. Her book has 90 pages. How many pages are left?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Solve one step, then use that result for the next step: 90 - (28 + 26) = 36.

questions.prompt: A jar held 75 buttons. 29 were blue and 18 were red. How many buttons were another color?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: Solve one step, then use that result for the next step: 75 - 29 - 18 = 28.

question_audio.text: A jar held 75 buttons. 29 were blue and 18 were red. How many buttons were another color?
question_audio.label: Read Question

level_banks.label: Mixed
level_banks.focus: Mixed addition, subtraction, compare, and two-step word problems

questions.prompt: Mia has 24 shells. Leo gives her 18 more. How many shells does Mia have now?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: 24 + 18 = 42. Add to put together or find the missing addend.

question_audio.text: Mia has 24 shells. Leo gives her 18 more. How many shells does Mia have now?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: There are 36 red apples and 27 green apples. How many apples are there altogether?

Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: 36 + 27 = 63. Add to put together or find the missing addend.

question_audio.text: There are 36 red apples and 27 green apples. How many apples are there altogether?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: There were 72 stickers. Ana used 28. How many stickers are left?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: Subtract to take apart or find the missing part: 72 - 28 = 44.

question_audio.text: There were 72 stickers. Ana used 28. How many stickers are left?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: A jar had 65 marbles. 19 rolled out. How many marbles stayed in the jar?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: Subtract to take apart or find the missing part: 65 - 19 = 46.

question_audio.text: A jar had 65 marbles. 19 rolled out. How many marbles stayed in the jar?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Ravi has 46 stamps. Kim has 29 stamps. How many more stamps does Ravi have than Kim?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: Compare by subtracting the smaller amount from the larger amount: 46 - 29 = 17.

question_audio.text: Ravi has 46 stamps. Kim has 29 stamps. How many more stamps does Ravi have than Kim?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: A team has 37 soccer balls and 58 basketballs. How many fewer soccer balls are there?

Choose add, subtract, or compare.

questions.prompt: A class collected 24 cans Monday and 31 cans Tuesday. They recycled 18 cans. How many cans are left?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: Solve one step, then use that result for the next step: 24 + 31 - 18 = 37.

question_audio.text: A class collected 24 cans Monday and 31 cans Tuesday. They recycled 18 cans. How many cans are left?
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Ben had 70 stickers. He gave 25 away and then got 14 more. How many stickers now?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: Solve one step, then use that result for the next step: 70 - 25 + 14 = 59.

story.text: Listen to this page. You are starting a math mission about Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Look at the story and think about what the characters need to solve. Math missions are easier when you name the numbers and the model first. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson teaches Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Look at the word problem model and connect each part to the math words. Use the vocabulary on the page to explain your thinking. Remember to check place value, units, or symbols before you answer. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This page lets you watch a worked example for Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Follow the model one step at a time. Say what each number means before you look at the final answer. If a symbol appears, read it in words like plus, minus, equals, greater than, or less than. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo helps you try Addition and Subtraction Word Problems with support. Build or study the model first, then connect it to the question. A good strategy is to explain the answer in math words before choosing. Press Next when you are ready for practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is practice. Use the visual model to help you think about Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This is a challenge. Use the visual model to help you think about Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This is your checkpoint. Use the visual model to help you think about Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your mission work on Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Look at your stars and remember the strategy you used. Mistakes help show what to practice next. Save your growth data to continue.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your growth for Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Look at attempts, correct answers, accuracy, and hints used. Use the recommendation to decide what to practice next. You can continue to Skill Practice or replay the mission.
profile.label: Read This Page
